source.description: Six-panel generated pencil process sheet cropped into step frames and finished art.
source.notes: Chosen for the June 29 Wimbledon hook; no reused sister-site subject or art.

process_strategy: The racket oval, handle, strings, ball, and ground shadow are introduced before the final panel so the finish only strengthens graphite lines and restrained tennis-ball color.

final_elements.name: tilted racket head oval
final_elements.description: A large slightly tilted oval frame for the tennis racket head.

final_elements.name: racket throat and wrapped handle
final_elements.description: A narrow throat below the oval leading into a long wrapped grip.

final_elements.name: inner rim and string grid
final_elements.description: The inner rim and simple crossed racket strings inside the head.

final_elements.name: tennis ball with curved seam
final_elements.description: A round tennis ball beside the racket with one curved seam line.

final_elements.name: wrap bands and soft cast shadow
final_elements.description: Grip wrap bands and a pale shadow anchoring the racket and ball.

final_elements.name: restrained yellow ball color
final_elements.description: Light yellow colored-pencil tone on the ball over the already drawn circle.

frames.visible_job: Draws the tilted racket head oval and a light center guide.
Adds the racket throat and long handle below the head.
Adds the inner rim and a simple visible string grid.
Places the tennis ball and its curved seam beside the racket.
Adds grip wrap bands, a soft cast shadow, and light yellow ball color.
introduces: wrap bands and soft cast shadow

final_step.visible_job: Only darkens keeper contours, clarifies strings, deepens handle wraps, and adds restrained shading to established shapes.

rejection_checks: No racket strings, ball seam, grip wrap, or color should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
